articles.title: Uncertainty in Strait of Hormuz as ceasefire deadline approaches

children.content: There is an intense uncertainty and volatility on the vital shipping waterway which is at the centre of a growing energy crisis.

image.altText: A large red tanker ship floats in blue waters near a smaller, blue ship.

articles.title: Iran's reclosure of the Strait of Hormuz 'disappointing', Marles says

children.content: The deputy prime minister says "every diplomatic angle" should be used to secure a permanent ceasefire in the Middle East.

image.altText: Close up of Richard Marles wearing a suit and tie.

articles.title: The world faces a grim reality, even if Trump exists in a different one

children.content: The world is stuck watching a performative series of announcements about meetings that could, may, will happen, involving a president not dealing with the reality that he is in a weak and confused bargaining position.

image.altText: Donald Trump stands outside the White House as reporters hold microphones.

children.content: Donald Trump says US negotiators are now having "very good" conversations with Iran that are "working out really well", even though shipping has again come to a standstill, with Iranian armed forces reportedly turning back two tankers attempting to pass through the strait. Follow live.

items.title: Trump announces new round of Iran talks as Strait of Hormuz traffic halts

updates.title: Iran won't send team to Pakistan, state media reports
Donald Trump  says US officials will be in Pakistan for negotiations
Iran prevents two tankers from transiting Strait of Hormuz, Iranian media reports

items.title: Donald Trump might have annoyed the only ally who can help clear sea mines

children.content: The prospect of mines in the Strait of Hormuz has highlighted a gap in US naval capabilities and reinforced its need for NATO help.

image.altText: A ship sails with others in the Gulf of Oman.

items.title: Man faces Melbourne court after pedestrian death at comic convention

children.content: Awer Dau, 33, faces the Melbourne Magistrates' Court on Sunday evening, charged with culpable driving causing death. He allegedly hit a 20-year-old man while driving outside the Supanova Comic Con and Gaming convention on Saturday.

image.altText: Bunches of flowers against a fence.

items.title: Could Australia make enough biofuel to keep us flying?

children.content: Australia exports billions of dollars worth of canola seed and corn husks to make sustainable jet fuel that is sold back to us. This is what it will take to make it here.

A large and bright yellow field of blooming canola, with trees like islands popping out of the sea of yellow

items.title: Ben Roberts-Smith issues first statement after his release on bail

children.content: War veteran Ben Roberts-Smith issues his first statement since his release on bail from a Sydney jail.

image.altText: Ben Roberts-Smith wearing a white shirt

items.title: Schools deemed safe after WA's biggest lithium-ion battery fire

children.content: Schools in Perth's south-east have been deemed safe for students to attend after WA's biggest lithium-ion battery fire sparked a HAZMAT warning in Maddington, which is still in place.

image.altText: Flames can be seen billowing from a building in an industrial area.

items.title: The myths of motherhood and the damage they do

children.content: I traced my family's maternal line from a home for abandoned children in Italy to my own wheelchair, confronting the myths we cling to about what makes a good mother — and the damage they do.

gallery.altText: Silhouette of a mother and young child facing away from each other with an image of an old Italian city in the background

thumbnail.altText: Silhouette of a mother and young child facing away from each other with an image of an old Italian city in the background

items.title: Demons cling on for epic win over Lions as Freo makes light work of Eagles

children.content: Melbourne digs deep to complete a thrilling two-point victory after a sensational tussle with Brisbane, while Fremantle claims Western Derby bragging rights with a 56-point humbling of West Coast.

image.altText: An AFL player in red and blue celebrates a goal with teammates

items.title: Eels stun Bulldogs after having spirit and desire publicly questioned

children.content: One week after their coach publicly questioned their spirit and desire, Parramatta produces a boilover to beat the Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs.

image.altText: Two NRL players celebrate with each other after a scoring a try

items.title: Eddie Nketia runs 100m faster than any Australian ever, but it doesn't count

children.content: Newly minted Australian sprinter Eddie Nketia breaks the national all-conditions 100m record set 23 years ago by Patrick Johnson.

image.altText: Eddie Nketia runs in a college sprint race ahead of another runner.

items.title: Newcastle Jets crowned A-League premiers with help from arch rivals

children.content: Newcastle has claimed a maiden A-League Men's Premiers Plate after local rivals Central Coast stunned second-placed Auckland.

image.altText: Soccer players wearing white celebrate a goal with a group hug

items.title: Fuel prices no obstacle as passionate photographers hit the road to chase auroras

children.content: For Leonie and Dean McFaull, fuel prices aren't stopping them crossing the country to add to their capture of more than 100 auroras.

items.title: Couple says loophole allowing theft from cars shipped interstate

children.content: Elihu and Amanda Del Valle say thousands of dollars worth of their belongings were stolen from the boot of their car when it was shipped from Melbourne to Perth, but police aren't able to investigate.

image.altText: A close up photo of a man with dark hair, wearing a black top

items.title: Police investigate separate alleged murders near Newcastle

children.content: A man charged with an alleged domestic violence murder of a woman, believed to be his mother, is refused bail. Meanwhile, police are calling for public help after a man was stabbed by masked intruders at Argenton.

image.altText: two police vehicles out the front of a home

items.title: We already loved each other, but just didn't realise it

children.content: After a casual fling led to a surprise pregnancy in my mid-20s, I was parenting alone. A phone call two years later changed everything.

Tessa Dijkgraaf  holding her baby Memphis
Tessa with Jordan, Memphis and their dog

items.title: The cast of Good News Week reflect on 30 years of the comedy game show

children.content: On April 19, 1996, comedy news game show Good News Week premiered, hosted by Paul McDermott, sparking nine seasons, a Gold Logie nomination and some first-chances for comedians.

image.altText: Paul McDermott, 30s, Julie McCrossin, 40s, and Mikey Robins, 30s, pose together on a stage littered with papers.

items.title: Doing disco or school pick-up, Jessie Ware is having more fun than ever

children.content: Whether making opulent, grown-up disco-pop or doing school pick-up, the glam British artist is having more fun than ever.

image.altText: Middle-aged brunette woman wearing fashionable skirt, bra & chain top poses with right hand upright

items.title: Who's really behind 'Big Australia'? Taylor's high immigration stance raises eyebrows

children.content: Angus Taylor's criticism of high immigration puts a strange spotlight on his own party's policy legacy.

image.altText: Angus Taylor stands in the courtyard of Parliament House with a TV camera pointed at him.

items.title: The quiet disappearance of Australia's rainfall observation network

children.content: For more than 60 years, Frank Pritchard has diligently checked the weather, adding to a record that stretches back more than a century. But he's part of a quietly shrinking network of observers around Australia. Without it, scientists say we risk missing out on important information that could help improve weather forecast models and climate projections.

image.altText: An older man with balding white hair holds up a rain gauge.

items.title: How Trump turned on the first US-born Pope

children.content: US President Donald Trump described Pope Leo XIV's accession to the papacy as a meaningful moment for America. But now he has said the pontiff is hurting the Catholic Church, and is weak. Here's how their relationship has developed over the past year.

image.altText: A graphic photo of both Donald Trump and Pope Leo.

items.title: Survivors of institutional abuse in Queensland want this loophole closed

children.content: Survivors of institutional abuse are calling on the state to follow other states in closing a legal loophole stopping victims from seeking justice.

image.altText: A sepia photo of a young girl

items.title: Top national park in NSW revealed as state hits record number of visits

children.content: The latest NSW National Parks and Wildlife Service Park Visitation Survey shows there were 65.5 million visits to the state's parks over the 2024-25 financial year.

gallery.altText: a buchwalking group at the Blue Mountains National Park

thumbnail.altText: a buchwalking group at the Blue Mountains National Park

items.title: Andrew and Tristan Tate's renewed defamation claim names Australian YouTuber

children.content: An Adelaide YouTuber has been identified in a renewed defamation lawsuit self-described misogynist Andrew Tate and his brother Tristan have lodged with a US court against some of their online critics.

image.altText: Andrew Tate wearing sunglasses walks with brother Tristan

items.title: Communities 'in the middle of nowhere' are scared they'll run out of fuel

children.content: Remote communities are calling for assurance on fuel security in the face of potential shortages, along with financial assistance as already-high fuel and food prices continue to climb.

image.altText: Alastair King stands at the checkout register of a small grocery store, speaking to the worker behind the counter.

items.title: Six dead after gunman opens fire and barricades supermarket in Kyiv

children.content: At least six people have been killed and 14 are injured after a gunman opened fire in the streets of Kyiv.

image.altText: A police officer holds his hand out in front of the supermarket door.

items.title: The real population of this remote opal town is anyone's guess

children.content: As the 2026 census approaches, residents in Lightning Ridge fear inaccurate calculations of their population will affect the provision of essential services.

image.altText: Sign reads 'Lightning Ridge population ?"

items.title: The mystery of an outback 'catch and kill' squad during WWII has been solved

children.content: In the 1940s, a shadowy military unit was assembled to catch and kill Japanese soldiers on Australia's remote northern coast. The identity of the so-called Solomons Six was a mystery — until now.

gallery.altText: Four back and white headshots of men dressed in army uniform.
A black and white photo of a large group of men standing to attention in the bush
A black and white photo of a group of men studying pictures of aeroplanes on the deck of a boat.

items.title: Hospital staff using ChatGPT to calculate medication doses, nurses claim

children.content: Whistleblowers say understaffing at the Northern Territory's busiest hospital is forcing nurses to take on unsafe patient loads, and some are teaching themselves medical procedures via YouTube.

image.altText: A nurse in uniform walking through the corridor of a hospital, facing away from the camera.

items.title: Out at sea, 400 million barrels of oil buffer Japan from the fuel crisis

children.content: Japan has oil stockpile sites littered around the country, and as the world faces a fuel crisis, neighbours in Asia have been turning to Tokyo for assistance.

image.altText: An aerial view shows eight barges floating in the water just off Japan.

items.title: 'No good reason' for schools to post photos of students on social media: expert

children.content: The independent girls' school in Brisbane is now only sharing what it says are unidentifiable shots of students' faces from the side or behind, due to concerns about image manipulation.

image.altText: Unidentifiable photos of students in green uniforms.

items.title: How a park on the Murray carries our bonds with the Western Front

children.content: A local Australian artist is exploring the bonds between a regional park and its namesake in France as part of an Anzac Day exhibit.

image.altText: Black and white photograph of Albury soldiers standing in front of a French building

items.title: Why statue of sex worker 'Joy' was attacked with a hammer

children.content: During her short residency in Sydney, the statue of sex worker Joy had many detractors and was even vandalised. Thirty years later, she has returned, with her supporters hoping she will dismantle stereotypes that still plague the industry.
